title: Make Podman-managed bind mounts unbindable
body: This helps prevent potential mount loops when a user bind-mounts a large portion of the host (including bits that Podman uses) into a container using a shared mount propagation.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Does this help https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1886827 - podman: containers fail to start with error "no space left on device" due to huge number of nested bind mounts (/run/.containerenv and /run/secrets) in container host's mount namespace
It may. I have not yet confirmed that it resolves the issues.
@mheon, which bind is being made unbindable here? "/run"? I mean makeing "/run" private should be enough to make sure that any mounts under it by container do not propagate to host.
If we are making a mount unbindable after the mount "/run/secrets", I think its already too late because it will have propagated to host anyway by then

@rhvgoyal This should make all bind mounts of single files that are directly managed by Podman (`/etc/hosts`, `/etc/resolv.conf`, `/etc/hostname`, `/run/.containerenv`, and a few more I'm forgetting) use `unbindable`. It doesn't touch `/run/secrets` yet, which lives in a separate place.

I think this is probably more correct than `private` propagation, even if it does not resolve the given BZ. I do want to verify that VM-based runtimes like Kata don't mind the mount option, though, it's a little atypical.
I'll make a separate PR for the secrets mounts next week, and do more complete testing to see if this actually does resolve the situation.

> @rhvgoyal This should make all bind mounts of single files that are directly managed by Podman (`/etc/hosts`, `/etc/resolv.conf`, `/etc/hostname`, `/run/.containerenv`, and a few more I'm forgetting) use `unbindable`. It doesn't touch `/run/secrets` yet, which lives in a separate place.
> 
> I think this is probably more correct than `private` propagation, even if it does not resolve the given BZ. I do want to verify that VM-based runtimes like Kata don't mind the mount option, though, it's a little atypical.

IIUC, "unbindable" says a mount is private. So submount under is will not propagate. Also unbindable will make sure that it can't be bind mounted.

So if you are mounting a file inside container at "/etc/hosts", and then making it unbindable, what problem is this solving. Are you trying to prevent somebody else from bind mount /etc/hosts further?

I am just not able to understand what problem you are solving and how unbindable is solving it. That's why asking all these questions, sorry.
Looking more into this, I think you're right - this won't fix our issue. Closing.
